danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites appearing on-screen in jungle and tunnel stages
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat requiring immediate player response

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible obstacles in jungle terrain during Stage 1
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard that damages player on contact
Long-range threat requiring evasion and cover awareness

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited visibility in Stage 3 bunker without flares
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental obscuration prevents enemy detection; flares required to reveal threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark shapes visible against background in bunker stage
danger_signals.meaning: Skilled players can identify enemies through environmental contrast even without flares

safety_signals.visual_form: Collected items from tunnel system stage
safety_signals.meaning: Resource enabling visibility and enemy detection in bunker

safety_signals.visual_form: Navigation tool obtained in Stage 1 town
safety_signals.meaning: Reduces disorientation and guides player through tunnel system

safety_signals.visual_form: Navigation item collected in Stage 2 tunnel system
safety_signals.meaning: Orientation aid enabling navigation through jungle Stage 4
Shelter providing protection from siege; player must defend location

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player character sprite in jungle combat gear
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bunker positions, bridge locations serving as military objectives

faction_indicators.faction: Enemy forces / Viet Cong and North Vietnamese
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy soldier sprites throughout jungle and tunnel stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle terrain, tunnel system network, brick bunker positions (Sergeant Barnes stronghold)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss character positioned in brick bunker fortification
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bunker at Stage 4 jungle endpoint; represents command authority of enemy forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Side-scrolling view with dense vegetation, visible mines, scattered booby traps; player navigates vertical and horizontal space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage player in jungle environment; player collects explosives and supplies before bridge assault

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person perspective tunnel network; confined corridors requiring map navigation; reduced visibility without flares
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy spawns require rapid elimination; player collects navigation items (compass, flares) while advancing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person defensive position; darkness broken only by flare illumination; enemy silhouettes appear at perimeter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Passive siege scenario where player must survive enemy assault; flare use reveals enemy positions outside bunker; siege ends when time expires

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Third-person jungle view with 4-minute time limit indicator; compass visible as navigation aid; sniper fire incoming; brick bunker visible at stage endpoint
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player pursues Sergeant Barnes through jungle under sniper threat; boss fight requires five grenade hits to defeat; compass usage enables efficient pathfinding

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Settlement environment transitioning from jungle Stage 1; torch and map items visible for collection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resource gathering phase; player acquires mission-critical equipment (torch, map) before tunnel system entry

environmental_storytelling.technique: Explosive mines scattered in jungle terrain
environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 1 jungle littered with visible mine indicators communicating combat zone hazard level and enemy fortification strategy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Tunnel system complexity requiring collected map
environmental_storytelling.example: Underground passages communicate logistical challenge of moving through enemy-controlled territory; map is essential survival tool rather than convenience

environmental_storytelling.technique: Bunker siege scenario with visibility mechanics
environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 3 darkness and flare-dependent visibility conveys psychological pressure of defensive warfare and sensory deprivation under assault
Shift from side-scrolling to first-person to third-person perspective communicates different operational phases: infiltration, underground movement, defensive holdout, pursuit
Sergeant Barnes positioned in brick bunker at jungle terminus signals final opposition and command structure of enemy forces
4-minute countdown communicates urgency of final mission phase and resource scarcity under combat conditions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appear on-screen and attack player; continuously spawn in tunnel system; must be eliminated quickly to advance
Attack bunker from exterior; visible only as silhouettes in darkness; reveal positions when illuminated by flares; cease attack after time duration expires
Remains in brick bunker; shoots at approaching player; requires five grenade hits to defeat; represents final enemy objective
Navigates cautiously through mined jungle; collects explosives; plants charges on bridge objective
Engages in continuous combat; collects navigation aids (flares, compass); uses map to navigate tunnel system
Remains stationary in bunker; uses flares to detect external threats; shoots incoming enemies; survives time-limited assault
Moves through jungle under time constraint; evades sniper fire; uses compass navigation; engages boss in final confrontation

notes: Platoon NES adapts 1986 war film through action gameplay with limited faction visual differentiation. Game communicates world state primarily through environmental hazard density, visibility mechanics (flares, darkness), and perspective shifts rather than faction colors or symbols. Danger signals emphasize environmental threats (mines, traps) and enemy presence density. Safety signals are functional items (map, compass, flares) enabling progression. World state transitions marked by distinct visual presentations and gameplay mechanics: jungle infiltration (side-scrolling), tunnel transit (first-person), bunker defense (first-person with light mechanics), pursuit (third-person). Film tie-in adaptation notes indicate developer prioritized varied gameplay over strict narrative consistency. Stage 3 bunker mechanics most effectively communicate danger through sensory deprivation (darkness requiring flares for enemy visibility). Enemy faction identity implicit through opposition role rather than explicit visual markers.
